items.title: The devil wears Dolce: Streep, Tucci front row at Dolce and Gabbana fashion show
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Meryl Streep and Stanley Tucci sat front row at Dolce and Gabbana fashion show in Milan to film "The Devil Wears Prada" sequel. (Sept. 27)

items.title: Iran's top official urges regional unity against Israel as he visits allies in Lebanon
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A top Iranian security official called on regional countries on Saturday to put their difference aside and cooperate closely as they face what he called “Israel’s conspiracies.” Ali Larijani, head of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council, spoke in Beirut where he arrived earlier Saturday to attend the anniversary of Israel’s assassination of Hezbollah leader Hassan Nasrallah in Lebanon.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.content:

items.title: Addressing climate change through mental health
items.author: Associated Press
items.content: <article>When it comes to tackling climate change, where should we start? One group at New York Climate Week says with mental health.</article>

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Arnold Schwarzenegger took up the baton at Munich’s Oktoberfest, where the former Republican governor of California spontaneously conducted a live music band and the singing crowd in a beer tent on Friday night.

items.title: Iraq resumes oil exports from the Kurdish region to Turkey after a halt of more than 2 years
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Iraq resumed exporting oil from the semi-autonomous northern Kurdish region through Turkey’s Ceyhan port on Saturday, after more than two years, officials said.

items.title: Seychelles president seeks a second term as people vote in African tourist haven
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The people of Seychelles voted Saturday in an election to choose a new leader and parliament, with President Wavel Ramkalawan seeking a second term in Africa’s smallest country. (AP video by Emilie Chetty)

items.title: Pakistan vaccinates around 9 million girls against HPV virus that causes cervical cancer
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Pakistan has vaccinated about 9 million adolescent girls against the virus that causes cervical cancer, as part of a continuing national campaign that has overcome early setbacks fuelled by skeptics online. (AP video shot by Muhammad Manaf)

items.author: Associated Press
items.content:

items.title: Displaced Palestinians in Gaza blast Netanyahu's UN speech
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Palestinians who recently fled Gaza City to areas in the south, blasted Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's speech at the United Nations Friday saying that the 23-month war in Gaza should stop.
